claim: Apple transitioned to year-based OS naming starting with WWDC 2025, so the third major release of visionOS was called 'visionOS 26' rather than 'visionOS 3'.

provenance.definition: Apple transitioned to year-based OS naming starting with WWDC 2025, so the third major release of visionOS was called 'visionOS 26' rather than 'visionOS 3'.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At WWDC 2025, Apple announced visionOS 26 as the successor to visionOS 2, adopting year-based naming for all major OS releases.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro, introducing new spatial computing features.

claim: Reports indicate Apple has effectively abandoned the Vision Pro platform, with stopped work on new models and team redistribution.

provenance.definition: Reports indicate Apple has effectively abandoned the Vision Pro platform, with stopped work on new models and team redistribution.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: MacRumors reported on April 29, 2026 that Apple has stopped work on Vision Pro, redistributed the team, and cancelled the Vision Air project.
cases.basis_quote: Apple has reportedly stopped work on Vision Pro, redistributed the team to other projects, cancelled the 'Vision Air' project, and shifted focus toward AI-powered smart glasses.

claim: Apple has historically announced major new versions of its operating systems at WWDC each June.

provenance.definition: Apple has historically announced major new versions of its operating systems at WWDC each June.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At WWDC 2024, Apple announced visionOS 2 as the first major update to visionOS.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 2, the first major update to the operating system for Apple Vision Pro, bringing new spatial computing capabilities.

cases.description: At WWDC 2025, Apple announced visionOS 26, continuing the annual major OS update cycle.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.
